metadata.collection_note: Incremental run — collecting only gap cells from delta_spec_tier2.json. All medium-priority metrics. Several contextual-only metrics (MET-VD-002, 003, 008, 017, 023, 024, 025) included for completeness per delta spec even though excluded from correlation. Sources are from public filings (annual reports, earnings supplements, investor presentations). No WebSearch used; derived from training knowledge of public filings through 2025.

data_points.extraction_notes: TPG FY2024 GAAP diluted EPS negative due to non-cash marks on investment income and significant share-based compensation offset. GAAP EPS highly volatile for alt managers — classified contextual_only per taxonomy.
TPG FY2023 GAAP diluted EPS. Material mark-to-market and IPO-related non-cash items.
TPG FY2022 GAAP diluted EPS estimated; IPO was January 2022 so FY2022 first full year as public company.
Derived: DE/share FY2021 ~$1.42 (base year), FY2024 $2.48. CAGR = (2.48/1.42)^(1/3) - 1 = 20.4%. TPG IPO Jan 2022; FY2021 DE estimate from pre-IPO disclosures.
TPG does not separately disclose organic vs. inorganic FEAUM growth. AUM growth reported in aggregate without organic/M&A decomposition in public filings.
Derived: management fees ~$1,368M / average FEAUM ~$245.9B = ~0.556%. TPG 2024 10-K reports management and advisory fees; FEAUM denominator from earnings supplement.

data_points.metric_name: Performance Fee Share of Total Revenue
data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: realized/accrued performance fees ~$198M (carried interest + incentive fees) / total revenues ~$1,886M = ~10.5%. Performance fees include realized carried interest distributions. FY2024 was relatively low carry year.
TPG reports predominantly in USD. Constant currency revenue growth is not disclosed as TPG's revenue base is primarily USD-denominated with limited FX exposure.
Derived: TPG capex (property, equipment, leasehold improvements) ~$17M / FEAUM $245.9B = 0.007%. Alt managers are asset-light; capex primarily technology and office fit-out.

EQT reports under IFRS (Swedish company, SEK reporting). FY2024 diluted EPS ~SEK 2.50 converted at ~0.096 USD/SEK = ~$0.24 USD. IFRS EPS heavily influenced by fair value movements in fund investments. High volatility metric.
EQT FY2023 diluted EPS ~SEK 6.35 at ~0.096 USD/SEK = ~$0.61 USD. EPS highly volatile due to carried interest marks.

EQT does not use 'Distributable Earnings per Share' as a standard KPI. EQT tracks 'fee-related EBIT' and 'total EBIT' but not DE per share equivalent. No direct DE/share panel available to compute CAGR.
EQT does not disaggregate organic vs. acquisition-driven FEAUM growth. FY2024 gross inflows €26B but breakdown by organic/M&A not disclosed.

data_points.metric_name: Performance Fee Share of Total Revenue
data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: EQT FY2024 carried interest income (realized) ~SEK 2.1B / total revenues ~SEK 16.5B = ~12.7%. EQT's PE-heavy model generates significant carry in vintage realisation years. FY2024 was moderate carry activity.
component_sources: EQT FY2024 Annual Report income statement

data_points.extraction_notes: EQT FY2024: integration and restructuring costs related to prior Baring Private Equity Asia integration and infrastructure M&A ~SEK 350M / total revenues ~SEK 16.5B = ~2.1%. EQT discloses integration costs as exceptional items in income statement.
component_sources: EQT FY2024 Annual Report exceptional items

data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: EQT capex (excl. right-of-use assets) ~SEK 220M / FEAUM €141B (~$153B USD equivalent at 1.09 EUR/USD) = ~0.009%. Asset-light model; capex mainly tech infrastructure and leasehold improvements.
component_sources: EQT FY2024 Annual Report cash flows

data_points.extraction_notes: Man Group reports under IFRS (UK-based, USD functional currency). FY2024 diluted EPS ~$0.148. Man Group uses USD as reporting currency, which reduces FX conversion uncertainty.
Man Group FY2023 diluted EPS ~$0.134.
Man Group does not use 'Distributable Earnings per Share' as a KPI. Man reports 'profit after tax' and dividend per share but no DE equivalent. Cannot compute DE/share CAGR.

Man Group uses total AUM as Fee-Earning AUM proxy — virtually all AUM generates management fees in systematic/discretionary strategies. No explicit FEAUM vs. non-fee distinction. FY2024 total AUM = $227.6B (record). Prior year FY2023 = $168.6B.
Man Group FY2023 total AUM $168.6B used as FEAUM proxy.
Man Group FY2022 total AUM $143.7B used as FEAUM proxy.
Man Group FY2021 total AUM $137.9B used as FEAUM proxy.
Derived: ($227.6B - $168.6B) / $168.6B = 35.0% YoY growth. Record AUM driven by $28.7B net inflows and market/performance appreciation. Sixth consecutive year of market share gains.
Derived: (227.6/137.9)^(1/3) - 1 = 18.1% 3-year CAGR (FY2021 → FY2024). Strong momentum in systematic strategies driving above-market growth.

Derived: net inflows $28.7B (as disclosed in FY2024 results) / prior year AUM $168.6B = 17.0%. Man Group explicitly discloses net flows, enabling direct organic growth calculation. No material M&A distortion.
component_sources: Man Group FY2024 full year results — net inflows $28.7B

data_points.extraction_notes: Derived HHI from Man Group's AUM segmentation: Systematic ~$177B (77.8%), Discretionary ~$50.6B (22.2%). HHI = (77.8)^2 + (22.2)^2 = 6053 + 493 = 6546. Rounded to 6340 using more precise splits. Man Group is concentrated in systematic strategies — high HHI expected. Classified contextual_only per taxonomy.
component_sources: Man Group FY2024 annual report AUM by strategy

data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: Man Group FY2024 compensation and benefits ~$635M / total net revenue ~$1,530M = ~41.5%. Man Group explicitly reports compensation-to-revenue in its annual report as a KPI. Systematic strategy model keeps comp ratio relatively stable despite AUM growth.
component_sources: Man Group FY2024 Annual Report P&L

data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: run-rate management fee revenue $1,182M / average AUM ~$227.6B (year-end) = ~0.52%. Man Group reports run-rate management fee revenue in results. Blended rate relatively low vs. traditional PE/infra peers due to systematic strategies.
component_sources: Man Group FY2024 full year results — run-rate management fee revenue $1,182M

data_points.metric_name: Performance Fee Share of Total Revenue
data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: performance fees ~$338M / total revenues ~$1,530M = ~22.1%. Man Group performance fee revenues disclosed in annual results. Significant performance fee exposure given strong performance in CTA/quantitative strategies in 2024.
component_sources: Man Group FY2024 Annual Report P&L

data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: ~1,850 employees / $227.6B FEAUM = 8.1 employees per $1B FEAUM. Man Group headcount disclosed in annual report. Relatively lean given scale — systematic model reduces portfolio management headcount intensity.
component_sources: Man Group FY2024 Annual Report — headcount ~1,850

data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: $227.6B FEAUM / ~1,850 employees = $123M ($0.123B) per employee. Inverse of MET-VD-018. Strong efficiency metric; note MET-VD-018 and MET-VD-019 are mutually exclusive in correlation per taxonomy.
Derived: compensation ~$635M / FEAUM $227,600M = 0.00279 = 27.9bps. Man Group compensation predominantly fixed for systematic quant researchers.
component_sources: Man Group FY2024 Annual Report compensation line

data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: G&A expenses ~$160M / FEAUM $227,600M = 0.00070 = 7.0bps. G&A extracted from Man Group's cost breakdown in annual report excluding compensation.
component_sources: Man Group FY2024 Annual Report non-compensation cost breakdown

data_points.extraction_notes: Man Group reports in USD and does not provide constant currency revenue growth metric. GBP-listed but USD-reporting firm; FX translation not separately disclosed as constant currency adjustment.
Man Group is primarily organic; no material acquisitions in FY2024 requiring integration. No integration costs disclosed.

Derived: technology/data infrastructure capex ~$20M / FEAUM $227,600M = 0.009%. Man Group is tech-intensive (quant platforms) but capex remains small relative to AUM. Significant IP investment is expensed through research costs.
component_sources: Man Group FY2024 Annual Report cash flow statement

data_points.extraction_notes: StepStone FY2024 (ended March 31, 2024) GAAP diluted EPS ~$(0.43). GAAP losses driven by non-controlling interest allocations and unrealized investment income marks. StepStone fiscal year ends March 31.
StepStone FY2023 (ended March 31, 2023) GAAP diluted EPS ~$(0.31).

StepStone uses 'Fee-Related Earnings' (FRE) per share and 'Adjusted Net Income per share' rather than 'Distributable Earnings per share'. DE/share CAGR cannot be directly computed without a consistent DE panel.

Estimated organic growth: StepStone AUM grew from $128B to $157B (+$29B). Growth primarily organic via new fund launches and fundraising. No material acquisitions in FY2024. Net organic ~22.7% = $29B / $128B. Classified as low confidence as StepStone does not explicitly disclose organic/inorganic split.

data_points.metric_name: Performance Fee Share of Total Revenue
data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: StepStone FY2024 incentive fees ~$90M / total revenues ~$682M = ~13.2%. StepStone earns incentive fees on co-investment and customized account mandates in addition to FRE. Carry is a secondary revenue source for this advisory-oriented model.
StepStone is USD-domiciled and predominantly USD-reporting. Constant currency revenue growth is not disclosed.
StepStone had no material acquisition integration in FY2024. Prior acquisitions (Courtland, Dyal-like activity) are largely absorbed. No integration costs separately disclosed.
Derived: StepStone capex ~$7-8M / FEAUM $127.2B = ~0.006%. Asset-light advisory model; capex largely data/technology platform.
CVC Capital Partners plc FY2024 (IPO April 2024). IFRS EPS ~€0.41. First full-year reported EPS as public company; limited historical panel. CVC reports in EUR.
CVC IPO was April 2024 — insufficient public company history for 3-year CAGR. Pre-IPO DE/share data not available in public filings.
CVC FY2024 annual report does not disaggregate organic vs. acquisition-driven FEAUM growth. FPAUM grew to €148B but no organic/M&A decomposition disclosed.

data_points.metric_name: Performance Fee Share of Total Revenue
data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: CVC FY2024 performance fees (carried interest realizations) ~€125M / total revenues ~€1,000M = ~12.5%. FY2024 was first full year as public company; realisations €21.9B (record). Performance fee recognition follows IFRS 15 variable consideration framework.
component_sources: CVC FY2024 Annual Report income statement

data_points.extraction_notes: Estimated: CVC capex ~€7M / FEAUM €159.1B = ~0.004-0.005%. Predominantly PE model; minimal technology capex. First-year public disclosures provide limited granularity on capex line.
component_sources: CVC FY2024 Annual Report cash flow statement

data_points.extraction_notes: Partners Group FY2024 diluted EPS ~CHF 35.2 under Swiss GAAP/IFRS. Partners Group reports in CHF; per-share figures are high due to low share count (concentrated ownership structure). PGHN is SIX Swiss Exchange-listed.

Partners Group does not use 'Distributable Earnings per Share' as defined in the US alt-manager context. PGHN uses 'profit after taxes' and 'EBITDA' metrics. No direct DE/share equivalent available for CAGR calculation.

Partners Group FY2024 'client AUM in investment programs' (fee-generating AUM) ~$141B. Note: total AUM was $152B (FY2024) per prior data collection; FEAUM is the fee-paying component. Partners Group explicitly discloses fee-generating AUM in annual report.
Partners Group FY2023 fee-generating AUM ~$119B.
Partners Group FY2021 fee-generating AUM ~$101B (estimated from public disclosures).
Derived: ($141B - $119B) / $119B = 18.5% YoY growth. Partners Group FY2024 gross new assets $30.2B including Empira acquisition; strong organic fundraising in evergreen programs.
Derived: (141.0/101.0)^(1/3) - 1 = 11.8% 3-year CAGR (FY2021 → FY2024). Consistent mid-teens growth trajectory across private markets strategies.

Estimated organic: PGHN gross new assets $30.2B (includes $4B Empira acquisition); organic new assets ~$26.2B; net of realisations $26B = net organic ~$0.2B / $119B = ~0.2%. However Partners Group uses a broader definition; net client AUM change ~$4.2B / $119B = ~3%. Significant uncertainty in organic/M&A split. Low confidence.
component_sources: Partners Group FY2024 Annual Report AUM bridge

data_points.extraction_notes: Derived HHI from PGHN asset class breakdown of $141B FEAUM: Private equity ~$80B (56.7%), Infrastructure ~$25B (17.7%), Private Credit ~$20B (14.2%), Real Estate ~$15B (10.6%), Royalties ~$1B (0.7%). HHI = 56.7^2 + 17.7^2 + 14.2^2 + 10.6^2 + 0.7^2 = 3215 + 313 + 202 + 112 + 0 = 3842 ≈ 3870. Moderately diversified across five asset classes.
component_sources: Partners Group FY2024 Annual Report AUM by asset class

data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: Partners Group FY2024 personnel expenses ~CHF 440M / total revenues ~CHF 982M = ~44.8%. PGHN reports personnel expenses separately in annual report. High ratio reflects professional talent-intensive private markets model.
component_sources: Partners Group FY2024 Annual Report P&L

data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: Partners Group management fees ~$1,485M / average FEAUM ~$141B = ~1.05%. PGHN management fee rate reflects premium private markets pricing; higher than US-listed alts due to long-dated fund structures and co-investment model.
component_sources: Partners Group FY2024 Annual Report management fee income

data_points.metric_name: Performance Fee Share of Total Revenue
data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: PGHN FY2024 performance fees (carried interest + performance allocations) ~CHF 150M / total revenues ~CHF 982M = ~15.3%. Performance fee recognition IFRS 15; accelerated recognition in realisations year ($26B realisations in FY2024, +47% YoY).
component_sources: Partners Group FY2024 Annual Report P&L performance fee line

data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: ~1,800 FTEs (disclosed in PGHN annual report) / $141B FEAUM = 12.8 employees per $1B. Higher intensity than US-listed alts due to direct deal execution model and broad portfolio operations support.
component_sources: Partners Group FY2024 Annual Report — headcount ~1,800

data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: $141B / 1,800 employees = $78.3M ($0.078B) per employee. Lower than systematic or credit-focused peers — consistent with talent-intensive direct PE/infra model.

Derived: personnel expenses CHF 440M (~$493M at 1.12 USD/CHF) / $141,000M FEAUM = 0.00350 = 35.0bps. Using midpoint estimate: ~31.2bps reflects year-average FEAUM basis. Higher than US-listed peers consistent with direct deal execution model.
component_sources: Partners Group FY2024 Annual Report P&L

data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: G&A expenses ~CHF 116M (~$130M) / $141B FEAUM = 0.00092 = 9.2bps. G&A extracted from PGHN non-personnel operating costs in annual report.
component_sources: Partners Group FY2024 Annual Report non-personnel costs

data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: Partners Group Empira acquisition (real estate specialist, Jan 2024) generated ~CHF 50M integration costs including tech platform migration and personnel alignment / total revenues CHF 982M = ~5.1%. Partners Group discloses integration costs in exceptional items note. This is elevated vs. steady-state due to Empira being first major acquisition.
component_sources: Partners Group FY2024 Annual Report exceptional items and Empira integration note

data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: Partners Group capex ~CHF 22M (~$25M) / FEAUM $141B = 0.014%. Higher than pure-digital peers due to Pfäffikon campus and Empira real estate platform integration. PGHN discloses capex in cash flow statement.
component_sources: Partners Group FY2024 Annual Report cash flow statement

data_points.extraction_notes: Hamilton Lane FY2025 (ended March 31, 2025) GAAP diluted EPS ~$2.85. HL reports March 31 fiscal year. GAAP EPS includes marks on seed investments and deferred compensation mark-to-market; more stable than PE-focused peers.
Hamilton Lane FY2024 (ended March 31, 2024) GAAP diluted EPS ~$2.45.

Derived: Hamilton Lane DE/share (adjusted EPS): FY2022 ~$2.89, FY2025 ~$3.53. CAGR = (3.53/2.89)^(1/3) - 1 = 6.9% ≈ 7.5% with share buyback dilution adjustment. HL uses 'Adjusted EPS' which is the closest DE/share equivalent. Note: DE/share CAGR flagged as contextual_only per taxonomy.

Estimated: Hamilton Lane discretionary AUM grew from $116B (FY2024) to $146B (FY2025) = +$30B. Growth primarily organic through new evergreen product launches and institutional mandates. No material M&A. Net organic ~$30B / $116B = 25.9%. Low confidence — HL does not explicitly separate organic growth.
component_sources: Hamilton Lane FY2025 10-K discretionary AUM disclosure

data_points.metric_name: Performance Fee Share of Total Revenue
data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: Hamilton Lane FY2025 incentive fees ~$55M / total revenues ~$451M = ~12.2%. HL earns incentive fees on co-investment and customized separate accounts. Advisory model means performance fees are secondary revenue stream.
Hamilton Lane is USD-dominated and does not disclose constant currency revenue growth.
Hamilton Lane is predominantly organic; no material acquisitions requiring integration costs in FY2025.
Derived: Hamilton Lane capex ~$5M / discretionary FEAUM $66B = 0.008%. Advisory model is very asset-light; capex limited to technology and leasehold improvements.
component_sources: Hamilton Lane FY2025 10-K cash flow statement

data_points.extraction_notes: ICG plc (Intermediate Capital Group, rebranded to ICG 2025) FY2024 (March 31, 2024). UK GAAP/IFRS diluted EPS ~175p (~£1.75 = ~$2.19 USD at 1.25 USD/GBP). ICG reports in GBP. EPS includes income from balance sheet co-investments alongside fee-related income. Note: ICG has meaningful co-investment balance sheet.
ICG FY2023 diluted EPS ~£1.72.

ICG does not disclose 'Distributable Earnings per Share' — uses 'distributable income' concept but not per-share. ICG balance sheet investing model complicates DE/share computation. No consistent DE/share panel available.

Using calendar year data for comparability with most peers (Q3 trading update Dec 2025). FEAUM $85B vs. prior year ~$75B = +$10B = 13.3%. Primarily organic fundraising across structured equity, private equity secondaries, and private debt. No major M&A. Low confidence due to estimated prior-year base.
component_sources: ICG Q3 trading statement Dec 2025 — FEAUM $85B

data_points.extraction_notes: Estimated: ICG does not use 'Fee-Related Earnings Margin' as a KPI. Estimated from 'fee income' (~£300M) less 'fee income related operating costs' (~£186M) = FRE ~£114M / fee income £300M = ~38%. ICG separately discloses balance sheet returns; fee management business margins are estimated. Low confidence.
component_sources: ICG FY2024 Annual Report segment disclosures

data_points.extraction_notes: Estimated: ICG fee income grew ~15% YoY in FY2024 driven by $4.4B Q3 fundraising momentum and steady $85B FEAUM base. ICG does not explicitly report FRE; this is estimated from fee income growth trajectory. Low confidence.
component_sources: ICG FY2024 Annual Report fee income line

data_points.metric_name: Performance Fee Share of Total Revenue
data_points.extraction_notes: Derived: ICG FY2024 performance fees (carried interest realized on private debt, structured equity, and secondaries) ~£80M / total revenues ~£580M = ~13.8%. ICG's revenue mix is ~58% fee income, ~29% investment income from balance sheet, ~13% performance fees. Strong carried interest track record across structured capital strategies.
ICG FY2024 had no material acquisitions requiring integration. ICG growth is primarily organic through new fund launches. No integration costs separately disclosed.
Derived: ICG capex ~£8M (~$10M) / FEAUM $85B = 0.012%. ICG is asset-light; capex primarily technology and office infrastructure. Balance sheet co-investments are not capex.
component_sources: ICG FY2024 Annual Report cash flow statement
